dublinCore.dc:title: 119 HR 7865 IH: American Consumer Tariff Rebate Act of 2026
dublinCore.dc:rights: Pursuant to Title 17 Section 105 of the United States Code, this file is not subject to copyright protection and is in the public domain.

action.action-desc: Mr. Cuellar introduced the following bill; which was referred to the Committee on Ways and Means

form.official-title: To provide direct refunds to taxpayers for increased consumer costs attributable to tariffs imposed without congressional authorization.

section.text: This Act may be cited as the American Consumer Tariff Rebate Act of 2026.

paragraph.text: Article I, Section 8 of the Constitution vests exclusively in Congress the authority to lay and collect duties and tariffs.

Duties imposed pursuant to Presidential action under the International Emergency Economic Powers Act (50 U.S.C. 1701 et seq.) without express congressional authorization resulted in increased consumer prices nationwide.
The Congressional Budget Office and the Joint Economic Committee have estimated that total consumer costs attributable to such tariffs are approximately $231,350,000,000.

Congress has a responsibility to provide direct restitution to taxpayers for these unlawful cost increases, and to prioritize working families by excluding very high-income taxpayers and using the resulting savings to increase refunds for taxpayers raising children.

The term covered tariffs means duties imposed pursuant to Presidential action under the International Emergency Economic Powers Act (50 U.S.C. 1701 et seq.) that were subsequently determined to lack congressional authorization.
The term eligible return means a Federal individual income tax return filed for the most recent taxable year for which sufficient information is available, including returns filed as—
The term qualified child has the meaning given in section 24(c) of the Internal Revenue Code of 1986.
The term Secretary means the Secretary of the Treasury or the Secretary’s delegate.

subsection.text: The Secretary shall provide a one-time direct payment to each Eligible Return in accordance with this section.
No payment shall be made under this Act with respect to any return reporting adjusted gross income exceeding $400,000 for the most recent taxable year for which sufficient information is available.
The total amount of payments made under this Act shall not exceed $231,350,000,000.

paragraph.text: The Secretary shall determine a Base Amount such that, if payments were made under subsection (e) with respect to all Eligible Returns (without regard to subsection (b)), the total amount of such payments would equal $231,350,000,000.
In calculating the Base Amount, the Secretary shall calculate such amount by dividing $231,350,000,000 by the sum of—

subparagraph.text: the number of Eligible Returns filed as single;
the number of Eligible Returns filed as married filing separately;
1.5 multiplied by the number of Eligible Returns filed as head of household; and

clause.text: the number of Eligible Returns filed as married filing jointly; and
the number of Eligible Returns filed as qualifying surviving spouse.

subsection.text: Subject to subsection (b), the payment for an Eligible Return shall be—

paragraph.text: in the case of a return filed as single, an amount equal to 100 percent of the Base Amount;
in the case of a return filed as married filing separately, an amount equal to 100 percent of the Base Amount;
in the case of a return filed as head of household, an amount equal to 150 percent of the Base Amount;
in the case of a return filed as married filing jointly, an amount equal to 200 percent of the Base Amount; or
in the case of a return filed as qualifying surviving spouse, an amount equal to 200 percent of the Base Amount.

section.header: Child bonus funded by high-income exclusion

subsection.text: In addition to any amount paid under section 4, the Secretary shall pay a Child Bonus with respect to any Eligible Return that—

paragraph.text: is not excluded under section 4(b); and
claims one or more Qualified Children.

subsection.text: The Child Bonus shall be equal to $125 for each Qualified Child claimed on such return.

paragraph.text: Amounts paid under this section shall be paid solely from amounts not paid due to the income limitation under section 4(b).
Total payments under this section, together with total payments under section 4, shall not exceed $231,350,000,000.

subsection.text: If the Secretary determines that the amount available under subsection (c)(1) is insufficient to pay the full Child Bonus amounts specified in subsection (b), the Secretary shall reduce each Child Bonus payment on a pro rata basis so that the aggregate limitation under section 4(c) is not exceeded.
The Secretary shall, to the maximum extent practicable, disburse the Child Bonus as part of the same payment as the section 4 rebate.
Payments shall be issued automatically using information available to the Internal Revenue Service.

paragraph.text: direct deposit;
paper check; or
prepaid debit card.

subsection.text: The Secretary shall establish a simplified filing procedure for individuals who did not file a return for the most recent taxable year but would otherwise have been eligible to file.
The Secretary may prescribe such guidance, regulations, and procedures as are necessary to carry out this Act.

The Secretary may round payment amounts to the nearest whole dollar or other administratively practical increment and shall implement such rounding in a manner that does not cause total payments to exceed $231,350,000,000.

section.text: Not later than 90 days after enactment, and every 60 days thereafter until all payments are distributed, the Secretary shall submit to Congress a report detailing—

paragraph.text: the number of payments issued by filing status under section 4;
the number of returns receiving a Child Bonus and the aggregate number of Qualified Children claimed for purposes of section 5;
the total amount disbursed under section 4 and under section 5; and
the remaining unobligated balance, if any, under the $231,350,000,000 aggregate limitation.
